message:
feat(string): clamp destination size for strlcpy / strlcat repair-mode

Threads explicit destination-bound awareness through strlcpy /
strlcat in hardened mode so a caller that passes a dstsize larger
than the allocation can be repaired by truncating rather than
blowing past the allocation and recording an unbounded overflow.

crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs:

- New clamp_destination_size_for_repair(requested, dst_remaining,
  repair) returning (effective_size, clamped) that encodes the
  rule: in repair mode, if the discovered destination bound is
  smaller than requested, clamp; in strict mode, do nothing. A
  #[doc(hidden)] pub fn clamp_destination_size_for_tests re-exports
  it for integration tests so the behaviour is directly
  verifiable.
- strlcpy / strlcat now use the clamped size when repair is
  enabled (heals_enabled || Repair action) and dst_bound is known,
  and record_truncation continues to observe the original
  requested size so telemetry matches what the caller asked for.

crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/string_abi_test.rs:

- strl_destination_bound_clamps_only_in_repair_mode exercises the
  (12, Some(5), true) / (12, Some(12), true) / (12, Some(5),
  false) / (12, None, true) quadrants so the clamping rule's full
  truth table is locked in.

tests/conformance/fixtures/string_ops.json:

- Adds strict_strlcpy_truncates_with_nul and
  strict_strlcat_truncates_with_nul cases so the baseline
  BSD-parity behaviour is covered alongside the pre-existing
  strcpy/strncpy/strcat fixtures.
- Adds hardened_strlcpy_dst_bound_clamps_requested_size and
  hardened_strlcat_dst_bound_clamps_requested_size which pass
  dst_alloc_len < requested_dstsize and expect
  repair=TruncateWithNull in the expected_output so the new
  clamp path shows up in the conformance report.

crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/string_ops_conformance_test.rs:

- string_ops_covers_strl_functions asserts both strict-mode
  coverage and hardened dst_bound repair fixtures exist for
  strlcpy and strlcat, and that the hardened fixture's expected
  output contains repair=TruncateWithNull. Prevents the fixture
  set from silently regressing to a world where strl hardened
  paths are only exercised at unit-test level.

files.patch:
@@ -17,11 +17,16 @@
     
     { "name": "strict_strcat_basic", "function": "strcat", "spec_section": "POSIX strcat", "inputs": { "dst_init": [65, 0], "src": [66, 0], "dst_cap": 5 }, "expected_output": "[65, 66, 0, 0, 0]", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "strict" },
     { "name": "hardened_strcat_overflow", "function": "strcat", "spec_section": "TSM hardened strcat", "inputs": { "dst_init": [65, 0], "src": [66, 67, 0], "dst_cap": 3 }, "expected_output": "[65, 66, 0]", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "hardened" },
+
+    { "name": "strict_strlcpy_truncates_with_nul", "function": "strlcpy", "spec_section": "BSD strlcpy", "inputs": { "src": [104, 101, 108, 108, 111, 32, 119, 111, 114, 108, 100, 0], "dst_len": 6, "dstsize": 6 }, "expected_output": "return=11,dst=[104, 101, 108, 108, 111, 0]", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "strict" },
+    { "name": "strict_strlcat_truncates_with_nul", "function": "strlcat", "spec_section": "BSD strlcat", "inputs": { "dst_init": [104, 101, 108, 108, 111, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0], "src": [32, 119, 111, 114, 108, 100, 0], "dstsize": 10 }, "expected_output": "return=11,dst=[104, 101, 108, 108, 111, 32, 119, 111, 114, 0]", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "strict" },
+    { "name": "hardened_strlcpy_dst_bound_clamps_requested_size", "function": "strlcpy", "spec_section": "TSM hardened strlcpy destination bounds", "inputs": { "src": [97, 98, 99, 100, 0], "dst_alloc_len": 4, "requested_dstsize": 8 }, "expected_output": "return=4,dst=[97, 98, 99, 0],repair=TruncateWithNull", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "hardened" },
+    { "name": "hardened_strlcat_dst_bound_clamps_requested_size", "function": "strlcat", "spec_section": "TSM hardened strlcat destination bounds", "inputs": { "dst_init": [97, 0, 0, 0], "src": [98, 99, 100, 0], "dst_alloc_len": 4, "requested_dstsize": 8 }, "expected_output": "return=4,dst=[97, 98, 99, 0],repair=TruncateWithNull", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "hardened" },
     
     { "name": "strict_strcmp_equal", "function": "strcmp", "spec_section": "POSIX strcmp", "inputs": { "s1": [65, 0], "s2": [65, 0] }, "expected_output": "0", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "strict" },
     
     { "name": "strict_strchr_found", "function": "strchr", "spec_section": "POSIX strchr", "inputs": { "s": [65, 66, 0], "c": 66 }, "expected_output": "Some(1)", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "strict" },
     { "name": "strict_strrchr_found", "function": "strrchr", "spec_section": "POSIX strrchr", "inputs": { "s": [65, 66, 65, 0], "c": 65 }, "expected_output": "Some(2)", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "strict" },
     { "name": "strict_strstr_found", "function": "strstr", "spec_section": "POSIX strstr", "inputs": { "haystack": [65, 66, 67, 0], "needle": [66, 0] }, "expected_output": "Some(1)", "expected_errno": 0, "mode": "strict" }
   ]
-}
\ No newline at end of file
+}
